name: GLM-5
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 744B total / 40B active, DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), 200K context, trained on a 100k-GPU Huawei Ascend 910B cluster (no Nvidia/AMD/Intel silicon)
note: Major-version flagship succeeding GLM-4.6; first Z.ai model released after Zhipu's Jan 2026 Hong Kong IPO; widely reported as trained entirely on domestic Huawei chips, no Nvidia used.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; Z.ai's own claimed 'leading open-model score' at launch
Terminal-Bench 2.0 (Terminus-2 harness); alt config reaches 60.7 per HF model card
Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools; with-tools variant reaches 50.4
BrowseComp, no context-management; with context-management reaches 75.9
tau2-Bench, labeled 'T2-Bench' on HF model card

IFBench (instruction-following), independently measured by Artificial Analysis, one of the Intelligence Evaluations shown on GLM-5 (Reasoning)'s live AA model page (72%, ranked 13th of 23 models shown). Not reported by Z.ai in its own launch materials (which cover HLE/AIME/GPQA/SWE-bench/Terminal-Bench/CyberGym/BrowseComp/tau2-bench, all already tracked) and not previously present in the corpus for GLM-5.

MCP Atlas score from the official Hugging Face model card's self-reported benchmark table. [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Verified via fully-rendered browser page (initial WebFetch/static-HTML pull returned stale zeroed placeholder values from Vals AI's animated counters).

verificationNotes:
This is a genuine, released model, not a rumor -- confirmed via zai-org/GLM-5 GitHub repo, Hugging Face model card, official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5, arXiv technical report 2602.15763 ('GLM-5: from Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering'), and extensive independent tech press (Let's Data Science, Pandaily-style outlets, Wikipedia's GLM(AI) article) all covering the Feb 2026 launch and its 'trained entirely on Huawei Ascend chips, zero Nvidia' angle, tied to Zhipu's Jan 8 2026 HKEX IPO. Release date: most sources say Feb 11, 2026; docs.z.ai's own release-notes timeline says Feb 12 -- one-day discrepancy, used the more commonly-cited Feb 11. Params: GitHub repo table and multiple press pieces agree on 744B total / 40B active; a couple of secondary blogs round to '745B/44B' -- treated as noise around the same figure, GitHub repo (self-published) used as primary. Benchmark scores for SWE-bench Verified (77.8) and Terminal-Bench 2.0 (56.2) confirmed directly on the official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5 page. HLE, AIME, GPQA-Diamond, CyberGym, BrowseComp, and tau2-Bench scores confirmed directly on the official Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-5), which lists a full self-reported benchmark table. Omitted SWE-bench Multilingual (73.3), BrowseComp-Zh (72.7), MCP-Atlas (67.8), Tool-Decathlon (38.0), and Vending Bench 2 ($4,432 balance) because they either lack a matching corpus slug or would require misfiling a distinct benchmark variant under an existing base slug.

[2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap-fill round] Researched five missing core-taxonomy benchmarks (mmlu, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu). LIVECODEBENCH: added at 81.87% via Vals AI's independent evaluation (vals.ai/models/zai_glm-5-thinking, displayed as 'GLM 5', release date 2/11/2026 matching this entry) -- see result notes. MMLU / MATH / HUMANEVAL: confirmed genuinely unpublished for the released model, not merely unfound. Fetched the official HF model-card raw README directly (huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5/raw/main/README.md) -- its one benchmark table (18 rows: HLE, AIME 2026 I, HMMT, IMOAnswerBench, GPQA-Diamond, SWE-bench Verified/Multilingual, Terminal-Bench 2.0 x2, CyberGym, BrowseComp x2, tau2-Bench, MCP-Atlas, Tool-Decathlon, Vending Bench 2) contains zero matches for MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval. Also fetched and full-text-searched the arXiv technical report (2602.15763v1) -- the only occurrences of these benchmark names are in Appendix B.1 'Evaluation of Base Models', Table 11, which reports MMLU (EM) 88.3, MATH (EM) 56.4, and LiveCodeBench-Base (Pass@1) 34.4 for GLM-5-Base -- the pre-RL/pre-post-training pretrained checkpoint compared against DeepSeek-V3-Base/Kimi-K2-Base/GLM-4.5-Base, NOT the RL-post-trained released 'GLM-5' model this corpus entry represents. Deliberately excluded these base-checkpoint numbers to avoid the variant-conflation error the corpus's own dataset audit flags as a top risk. Cross-checked independent trackers: Epoch AI has zero benchmark rows for GLM-5 at all (fetched epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv directly -- only a 'glm-5.2_max' row exists, 8 benchmarks none of which are MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/MMMU either); Vals AI's GLM-5 page tests a disjoint benchmark set (CorpFin v2, TaxEval v2, Vibe Code Bench, GPQA Diamond, IOI, LiveCodeBench, LegalBench, MMLU Pro, SWE-bench) that does not include base MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval. Cross-checked sibling family entries (GLM-5.1, GLM-5.2 Max/High, both in this corpus) -- none report mmlu/math/humaneval either, consistent with this being a family-wide, deliberate choice by Z.ai starting at GLM-5 (its predecessor GLM-4.5's own technical report DID publish self-reported MMLU=90/MATH-500=98.2 in a dedicated 'Commonly Used General Chat Benchmarks' table, confirming Z.ai actively dropped these from its GLM-5-generation public reporting rather than never having tested them). Considered LayerLens/Stratix (layerlens.ai, a real funded independent-eval startup) as a possible independent source but rejected its GLM-5 numbers as unreliable: its page mangles standard benchmark names (renders 'HumanEval' as 'Human Evaluation', 'GPQA' as 'General Purpose QA') and its own multi-batch results are internally inconsistent (HLE swings 22.41% to 10.37% across batches vs. every other source's consistent 30.5%), so no LayerLens figures were used anywhere in this pass. MMMU: this model is confirmed text-only both in this corpus file (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false) and in the arXiv technical report (no mention of vision/multimodal evaluation anywhere in the full paper text); MMMU requires image-input understanding, so its absence is a structural, expected outcome for a text-only model, not a research gap. No existing results were altered.

[2026-07-17 second-pass legacy-benchmark research round -- HELM/academic/GitHub/aggregator/non-English/sibling sweep] Chased mmlu, math, humaneval via the 6 mandated new-source-type checks (all 6 genuinely attempted, none limited to first-pass sources). Result: no credible number found for any of the three; all three remain omitted_no_evidence. Detail per source type:

1) STANFORD HELM: Direct crfm.stanford.edu/helm/{classic,lite}/latest/ fetches returned only static shell HTML (client-rendered SPA, no model data visible to a plain fetch). Worked around this via the EvalEval 'Every Eval Ever' project (github.com/evaleval/every_eval_ever, huggingface.co/datasets/evaleval/EEE_datastore), which ingests and mirrors current HELM run data (categories present: helm_lite, helm_classic, helm_mmlu, helm_capabilities, helm_instruct, helm_safety, helm_air_bench) alongside 90+ other benchmark/leaderboard sources, ~229K results / 22K models. Listed every organization directory under data/helm_mmlu (01-ai, ai21, allenai, amazon, anthropic, cohere, databricks, deepseek-ai, google, meta, microsoft, mistralai, openai, qwen, snowflake, upstage, writer) and data/helm_lite (same set plus AlephAlpha, tiiuae) -- no zai-org or zhipu-ai directory in either. data/helm_classic DOES have a zhipu-ai directory, but it contains exactly one model: GLM-130B (2022-era legacy model) -- confirming HELM has evaluated a Zhipu model before but never GLM-5. Conclusion: HELM (in any current variant) has not run GLM-5 on MMLU or any other task as of this pass.

2) ARXIV/SEMANTIC SCHOLAR BASELINE PAPERS: Searched "GLM-5 MMLU site:arxiv.org", "GLM-5 MATH benchmark arxiv baseline comparison table", "GLM-5 semantic scholar baseline comparison". Candidates fetched and checked: (a) arxiv 2306.02781v4 ('An Automated Survey of Generative AI' -- a continuously-revised living survey, last updated April 2026) mentions GLM-5's SWE-bench score but contains no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval figure for it despite discussing MMLU for other models (e.g. Gemini 3 Pro); (b) arxiv 2606.14516 ('Every Eval Ever: A Unifying Schema and Community Repository for AI Evaluation Results') does not mention GLM-5 in its own text at all (it's the paper describing the datastore checked in #3 below); (c) GLM-5V-Turbo paper (2604.26752, a Z.ai follow-on multimodal paper) not fetched in full but is a Z.ai self-paper, not an independent baseline citation. No qualifying third-party paper found that reports base MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval for the released (RL-post-trained) GLM-5 as a comparison baseline.

3) GITHUB EVAL-HARNESS RUN LOGS: The EEE_datastore (built from converted lm-evaluation-harness, HELM, and Inspect AI run logs) does contain zai-org/GLM-5 entries in two benchmark folders, both checked in detail and both rejected as variant mismatches rather than used: -- data/math-mc/zai-org/glm-5-fp8/ (file 19186bba-...json): this is 'MATH-MC', a multiple-choice reformatting of MATH from the paper Geralt-Targaryen/MC-Evaluation (arxiv 2405.11966), run by a third party ('Writer, Inc.', WASP v0.3.0 framework) on 2026-02-22. Scores are 98.6/98.4/99.2/99.4/99.1% by difficulty level -- far higher than free-response MATH-500 would read for the same model because multiple-choice format allows elimination-based guessing. This is a materially different construct from the corpus's 'math' slug (defined as MATH-500, 500 free-response competition problems), so using it would be exactly the variant-conflation error the corpus's own dataset audit flags as its top risk. Not used. -- data/mmlu-pro/zhipu-ai/glm-5/ (file 767d7365-...json): this is MMLU-Pro (TIGER-Lab's harder, 10-option, ~12K-question reformulation of MMLU, self-reported to the TIGER-Lab HF leaderboard, arxiv 2406.01574), scored 86%. MMLU-Pro is a distinct, harder benchmark from base 57-subject MMLU (the corpus's exact 'mmlu' slug) and the two are not interchangeable -- again would be variant conflation. Not used. -- Checked data/openai_humaneval/ (orgs present: anthropic, deepseek, google, meta-llama, mistral, mistralai, openai, qwen) and data/mbpp/ (anthropic, deepseek, google, grok, meta-llama, mistral, mistralai, openai, qwen) -- zero zai-org/zhipu-ai entries in either, so no HumanEval (or HumanEval-adjacent) run-log data exists for GLM-5 in this large community repository. Also checked data/global-mmlu-lite/ (alibaba, anthropic, cohere, deepseek, google, ibm, mistralai, openai, unknown, xai) -- no zai-org/zhipu-ai entry there either.

4) OTHER LEADERBOARDS/AGGREGATORS: Checked LiveBench.ai (no GLM-5-specific results surfaced in search), OpenRouter's GLM-5 and GLM-5.2 pages (list pricing/context/Artificial-Analysis-Index only, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval rows), Scale AI SEAL leaderboard (labs.scale.com/leaderboard -- only SWE-bench Pro coverage found for the GLM-5 family, no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval), and PapersWithCode-style searches (returned only the already-rejected LayerLens post and generic 2026 leaderboard aggregator sites, no PWC leaderboard page with a genuine GLM-5 row for these three benchmarks).

5) NON-ENGLISH SOURCES: Searched Chinese-language queries ("GLM-5 MMLU 智谱 评测", benchmark-specific variants) and Korean-language queries. Found only inconsistent, uncited numbers scattered across low-quality sites: zhihu.com posts (inaccessible -- 403 Forbidden on direct fetch, so their claimed numbers could not be verified against actual page content), ithome.com's Chinese tech-news launch article (fetched directly -- confirmed it does NOT report MMLU/MATH/HumanEval, only Artificial Analysis Index and LMArena mentions), and automatio.ai's GLM-5 profile page (fetched directly -- does list MMLU 88%, MATH 97.4%, HumanEval 97.0%, but with zero source attribution or methodology of any kind; these numbers also do not match each other across the various blogs found -- MMLU alone was variously quoted by different unverifiable aggregators as 88%, 89.2%, 91.72%, and 92.3% with no consistent primary source -- a pattern consistent with SEO content-mill noise/estimation rather than genuine measurement). None of these meets the bar of a credible, citable, real source required for an addition; none used.

6) SIBLING MODELS: Checked GLM-5.1's launch-comparison coverage (SWE-Bench Pro 58.4, NL2Repo 42.7, Terminal-Bench 2.0 63.5, CyberGym 68.7, AIME 2026 95.3, GPQA-Diamond 86.2 per multiple press summaries) -- no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval column anywhere in its comparison tables. Fetched the GLM-5.2 official Hugging Face blog (huggingface.co/blog/zai-org/glm-52-blog) directly and confirmed its 'Full Benchmark Table' covers HLE, CritPt, AIME, SWE-bench Pro etc. for GLM-5.2/GLM-5.1/competing frontier models, but contains no row at all for the original 'GLM-5' and no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval column for any model. No sibling-model comparison table anywhere provides a citable GLM-5 score for these three benchmarks.

Net conclusion: all 6 new source types were genuinely checked (exceeding the 3-type minimum). mmlu, math, and humaneval remain confirmed-absent for the released GLM-5 model -- this appears to be a deliberate Z.ai reporting choice for the GLM-5 generation (consistent with the prior round's finding that GLM-4.5's report DID publish MMLU/MATH-500 but GLM-5's does not), not a research gap. No existing results were altered; no new results added this round.

[2026-07-18 MMMU gap-chase round] Re-confirmed MMMU is architecturally inapplicable for this exact released GLM-5 model. Corpus file itself already states modalities: ["text"] and capabilities.vision: false. Re-verified directly against the two primary official sources (not relying solely on the prior round's cached conclusion): (1) fetched huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-5/raw/main/README.md fresh -- confirms the model card's own benchmark table (18 rows: HLE, HLE w/Tools, AIME 2026 I, HMMT Nov 2025, IMOAnswerBench, GPQA-Diamond, SWE-bench Verified/Multilingual, Terminal-Bench 2.0 x2, CyberGym, BrowseComp x2/-Zh, tau2-Bench, MCP-Atlas, Tool-Decathlon, Vending Bench 2) contains no MMMU row and no vision/image/multimodal mention anywhere; model is explicitly positioned as text-only. (2) fetched docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-5 fresh -- states Input/Output modalities as "Text"/"Text" only, no vision or MMMU mention. Both align with the corpus file's own capabilities.vision: false field and with the prior 2026-07-16 round's finding that the arXiv technical report (2602.15763) contains no vision/multimodal evaluation anywhere in its text.

Also ran a broad web search sweep ("GLM-5 MMMU benchmark score", "GLM-5 MMMU vision multimodal Zhipu Z.ai") per the research method's independent-tracker step, and it surfaced a cluster of low-credibility SEO/content-mill sites (glm5.ai, glm5.net, glm-5.org, layerlens.ai, automatio.ai, benchlm.ai, a GMI Cloud blog post) claiming GLM-5 is "natively multimodal" with fabricated MMMU scores (variously "84.3", or attributing GLM-5.1's unrelated "73%" figure to plain GLM-5). None of these are Zhipu/Z.ai-controlled domains (official are z.ai, docs.z.ai, github.com/zai-org, huggingface.co/zai-org) and layerlens.ai / automatio.ai were already flagged as unreliable in this file's prior verification round (mangled benchmark names, internally inconsistent scores across batches, no methodology disclosed). One source found in passing, GLM-5V-Turbo (arxiv 2604.26752), is a genuinely separate, later Z.ai multimodal model built on top of GLM-5 -- not the text-only GLM-5 this corpus entry represents -- and is not a valid source for this entry per the corpus's own variant-conflation rule. No credible independent tracker (Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, Artificial Analysis, Scale SEAL) shows an MMMU row for GLM-5 either -- Epoch AI's benchmarks.csv (checked in the prior round) has zero GLM-5 rows including MMMU, and Vals.ai/Artificial Analysis's GLM-5 pages (checked in this and prior rounds) do not include MMMU among their tracked evaluations.

Conclusion: MMMU absence is confirmed architecturally inapplicable (text-only model), not a research gap -- consistent across the corpus file's own capability flags, both primary official sources (HF model card, docs.z.ai), and the prior round's arXiv full-text check. No existing results altered; no new result added for mmmu.
